story.iWant: unuttuğum şifremi phone + SMS OTP ile reset edebilmek
story.tasks: - Task 1: ForgotPasswordDto oluştur (src/modules/auth/dto/forgot-password.dto.ts) - Task 2: ResetPasswordDto oluştur (src/modules/auth/dto/reset-password.dto.ts) - Task 3: AuthService.forgotPassword() method implement et (silent fail for non-admin) - Task 4: AuthController.forgotPassword() endpoint oluştur (POST /auth/forgot-password with rate limiting) - Task 5: AuthService.resetPassword() method implement et (OTP validation, password update, force re-login) - Task 6: AuthController.resetPassword() endpoint oluştur (POST /auth/reset-password) - Task 7: OtpService.generateOtp() extend et - purpose field support - Task 8: OtpService.validateOtp() extend et - purpose field support - Task 9: Unit test yaz - forgotPassword (admin/non-admin/non-existent scenarios) - Task 10: Unit test yaz - resetPassword (valid/invalid/expired OTP scenarios) - Task 11: Integration test yaz (full password reset flow, rate limiting, security tests)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: AC-2.6.1: POST /auth/forgot-password endpoint oluşturulmuş (public, authentication required değil) AC-2.6.2: Phone lookup + role check (admin only, silent fail for non-admin users) AC-2.6.3: OTP generated (6-digit) ve SMS sent (password reset template via FONIVA) AC-2.6.4: Response always 200 OK (security - phone existence disclosure yok) AC-2.6.5: Rate limiting: 3 attempts / hour per phoneNumber AC-2.6.6: POST /auth/reset-password endpoint oluşturulmuş (public) AC-2.6.7: OTP validated (exists, not expired, code match, attempts < 3) AC-2.6.8: New password validated (min 8 char, en az 1 harf ve 1 rakam) AC-2.6.9: Password hash updated in database (bcrypt) AC-2.6.10: All user refresh tokens deleted (force re-login on all devices) AC-2.6.11: Response: { success: true, message: "Password reset successful" }

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Comprehensive technical spec for password reset flow including OTP-based verification, silent fail pattern for security, admin-only restriction, and force re-login implementation details. Contains acceptance criteria AC-2.6.1 through AC-2.6.11, API specifications, data models, and security patterns.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: POST /auth/forgot-password (phoneNumber → OTP) and POST /auth/reset-password (phoneNumber + OTP + new password) endpoints. OTP-based verification for phone ownership proof. Rate limiting: 3 requests/hour per phone. Password policy enforcement.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: ForgotPasswordDto (@IsPhoneNumber, @IsNotEmpty for phoneNumber) and ResetPasswordDto (@IsPhoneNumber, @Length(6,6) for OTP, password validation with @Matches for min 8 char, letter+number requirement).

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.section: Workflows and Sequencing - Flow 5: Password Reset
doc.snippet: Two-step password reset flow: Step 1 - Forgot password (OTP generation and SMS sending with rate limiting), Step 2 - Reset password (OTP validation, password update, refresh token invalidation). Includes complete sequence diagrams and error handling patterns.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: User story definition: As an admin user, I want to reset my forgotten password using phone + SMS OTP, so that I can regain access to my account. Admin-only feature (staff users don't have passwords). FONIVA SMS integration required.

doc.title: Previous Story 2.5: Logout
doc.snippet: RefreshToken.deleteMany({userID}) pattern ready for force re-login. @Public() decorator available for public endpoints. Rate limiting infrastructure (@Throttle decorator) configured. Generic error messages pattern established for security (no disclosure). PrismaService patterns for user lookup, password update, and bulk token deletion working.

artifact.reason: Main auth controller where POST /auth/forgot-password and POST /auth/reset-password endpoints will be added. Existing endpoints show @Public() decorator pattern and @Throttle rate limiting usage.
Core auth service where forgotPassword() and resetPassword() methods will be implemented. Contains user lookup patterns, password hashing, and response formatting logic from previous stories.

Existing OTP service stub (Epic 5.1 placeholder). Needs full implementation with generateOtp(userID, purpose), validateOtp(userID, code, purpose), and deleteOtp() methods. Currently only has generateAndSendOtp() stub.
Token service with refresh token management. Contains patterns for token generation and database operations that can be referenced for understanding token invalidation flow.
Public decorator for marking routes that don't require JWT authentication. Required for both /auth/forgot-password and /auth/reset-password endpoints.
Reference for phone-based DTO structure with @IsPhoneNumber decorator. Pattern to follow for ForgotPasswordDto and ResetPasswordDto.
Contains password validation pattern (@Matches for min 8 char, letter+number). Same pattern needed for ResetPasswordDto.newPassword field.
Existing unit tests showing mock PrismaService patterns. Will be extended with forgotPassword() and resetPassword() test suites following established patterns.
E2E test pattern showing test database setup, user creation, token usage, and verification of database state changes. Pattern to follow for auth-password-reset.e2e-spec.ts.
E2E tests showing rate limiting verification (@Throttle decorator testing). Pattern needed for testing 3 attempts/hour rate limit on forgot-password endpoint.

story-context.constraints: - Silent Fail Pattern: forgot-password endpoint MUST always return 200 OK regardless of phone existence or user role to prevent enumeration attacks - Admin-Only: Password reset ONLY for users with role='admin' (staff users don't have passwords) - OTP Purpose Isolation: Extend OTPVerification model with purpose field ('login', 'password-reset', 'phone-verification') to prevent OTP cross-usage - Force Re-Login: After password reset, ALL refresh tokens for the user MUST be deleted (prisma.refreshToken.deleteMany({userID})) - Rate Limiting: forgot-password endpoint limited to 3 attempts per hour per phone (@Throttle decorator with ttl: 3600000ms) - OTP Security: 6-digit code (crypto.randomInt(100000, 999999)), 5-minute expiry, max 3 validation attempts - Password Policy: Min 8 characters, at least 1 letter and 1 number (@Matches regex validation) - Bcrypt Hashing: Password hashing with 10 rounds (bcrypt.hash(password, 10)) - Generic Error Messages: No credential disclosure in responses (both success and error cases) - Idempotent Operations: Database delete operations must be safe to retry - No Transaction: Atomic operations sufficient, no distributed transaction needed - Phone Format: E.164 format (+90XXXXXXXXXX) validated with @IsPhoneNumber('TR') - Response Format: Consistent with existing auth endpoints - { success: true, data/message: {...} } - Public Endpoints: Both endpoints marked with @Public() decorator (no JWT authentication required) - Epic 5.1 Dependency: SmsService integration for OTP sending (stub acceptable for now, real implementation in Epic 5.1)

interface.name: POST /auth/forgot-password
POST /auth/reset-password
AuthService.forgotPassword
interface.signature: async forgotPassword(phoneNumber: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; message: string; expiresIn: number }> - Lookup user by phone - Silent fail if user not found or not admin (return 200 OK) - Generate OTP with purpose='password-reset' - Send SMS via SmsService (Epic 5.1) - Return generic success message

interface.name: AuthService.resetPassword
interface.signature: async resetPassword(dto: ResetPasswordDto): Promise<{ success: boolean; message: string }> - Lookup user by phone - Validate OTP (purpose='password-reset') - Hash new password with bcrypt - Update user password in database - Delete OTP (cleanup) - Delete all refresh tokens (force re-login) - Return success message

interface.name: OtpService.generateOtp
interface.signature: async generateOtp(userID: string, purpose: OtpPurpose): Promise<string> - OtpPurpose = 'login' | 'password-reset' | 'phone-verification' - Invalidate previous OTPs for same user and purpose - Generate 6-digit code (crypto.randomInt) - Create OTP record with 5-minute expiry - Return OTP code

interface.name: OtpService.validateOtp
interface.signature: async validateOtp(userID: string, code: string, purpose?: OtpPurpose): Promise<OtpValidationResult> - OtpValidationResult = { valid: boolean, userID?: string, reason?: string, attemptsRemaining?: number } - Lookup OTP with optional purpose filter - Check expiry (< 5 minutes) - Check attempts (< 3) - Verify code match - Mark as verified or increment attempts - Return validation result

interface.name: OtpService.deleteOtp
interface.signature: async deleteOtp(userID: string, code: string): Promise<void> - Delete OTP records for cleanup after successful validation

interface.name: PrismaService.user.findUnique
interface.signature: prisma.user.findUnique({ where: { phoneNumber } }) - User lookup by phone number

interface.name: PrismaService.user.update
interface.signature: prisma.user.update({ where: { id }, data: { passwordHash } }) - Update user password hash

interface.name: PrismaService.refreshToken.deleteMany
interface.signature: prisma.refreshToken.deleteMany({ where: { userID } }) - Force re-login by deleting all refresh tokens for user

interface.name: PrismaService.otpVerification.create
interface.signature: prisma.otpVerification.create({ data: { userID, code, type: 'SMS', purpose, expiresAt, attempts: 0, verified: false } }) - Create OTP record with purpose field

interface.name: PrismaService.otpVerification.findFirst
interface.signature: prisma.otpVerification.findFirst({ where: { userID, code, purpose, verified: false } }) - Lookup OTP with purpose filter

interface.name: PrismaService.otpVerification.update
interface.signature: prisma.otpVerification.update({ where: { id }, data: { verified: true } }) - Mark OTP as verified

interface.name: PrismaService.otpVerification.deleteMany
interface.signature: prisma.otpVerification.deleteMany({ where: { userID, code } }) - Cleanup OTP after successful validation

tests.standards: Unit tests use Jest with mock PrismaService pattern (established in auth.service.spec.ts). E2E tests use supertest with test database setup (jest-e2e-setup.ts). All authentication flows must have both unit and E2E coverage. Testing pattern: Mock external dependencies, test business logic in isolation (unit), then test full flow with real database (E2E). Rate limiting tested by exceeding throttle limits and expecting 429 responses.
tests.locations: - Unit tests: src/modules/auth/auth.service.spec.ts (extend with forgotPassword and resetPassword test suites) - E2E tests: test/auth-password-reset.e2e-spec.ts (new file) - Test setup: test/jest-e2e-setup.ts (existing database setup)
tests.ideas: - AC-2.6.1, 2.6.4: Test forgot-password with admin user returns 200 OK and OTP sent message - AC-2.6.2, 2.6.4: Test forgot-password with non-admin user returns 200 OK (silent fail, no disclosure) - AC-2.6.2, 2.6.4: Test forgot-password with non-existent phone returns 200 OK (silent fail, no disclosure) - AC-2.6.3: Test OtpService.generateOtp() called with purpose='password-reset' - AC-2.6.3: Test SmsService.sendOTP() called with password reset template - AC-2.6.5: Test rate limiting on forgot-password (3 attempts per hour → 429 Too Many Requests) - AC-2.6.6, 2.6.11: Test reset-password with valid OTP updates password and returns success - AC-2.6.7: Test reset-password with invalid OTP throws BadRequestException - AC-2.6.7: Test reset-password with expired OTP throws BadRequestException - AC-2.6.7: Test reset-password with max attempts exceeded throws BadRequestException - AC-2.6.8: Test reset-password with weak password throws BadRequestException (class-validator) - AC-2.6.9: Test password hash updated in database (bcrypt verification) - AC-2.6.10: Test all refresh tokens deleted after password reset (force re-login) - E2E: Full flow - forgot password → receive OTP → reset password → login with new password succeeds - E2E: Old password fails after reset (401 Unauthorized) - E2E: Old refresh tokens invalidated after password reset (401 on /auth/refresh) - E2E: Multiple OTP validation attempts increment attempt count - E2E: OTP expires after 5 minutes - E2E: Purpose isolation - login OTP cannot be used for password reset
